beats.activity: Wake up, espresso ritual, check studio schedule and weather.
beats.reasoning: Monday's energy was high (45/100) but rain and SR-520 closure today mean a cautious start; Layla checks conditions before committing to Fremont studio time.

beats.activity: Commute to Fremont studio via local roads, avoiding SR-520 eastbound backup.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure 9am–noon forces a longer local route; leaving early avoids peak congestion and gets her to the studio before the worst rain hits.

beats.activity: Wheel-throwing and glaze testing in studio; deep focus work on new forms.
beats.reasoning: Rainy morning (7am–2pm rain) is perfect for indoor studio work; she can push her glaze experiments forward without weather interruption.

beats.activity: Quick lunch at Molly Moon's, staying dry indoors during peak rain.
beats.reasoning: Rain is heaviest 7am–2pm; staying in Fremont for a warm, sheltered lunch keeps momentum going without commuting.

beats.activity: Return to studio; continue wheel-throwing and prep pieces for weekend craft fair.
beats.reasoning: Rain easing after 2pm but still grey; focused afternoon work on fair inventory while indoors, leveraging workaholic energy to batch production.

beats.activity: Commute home via local streets; rain clearing.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure likely resolved by 5:45pm; Layla heads home as daylight fades and weather settles, ready for evening wind-down.

beats.activity: Grocery stop for dinner supplies and espresso beans.
beats.reasoning: Quick neighborhood errand before dusk; restocks essentials for the week and keeps her near home.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner and eat at home.
beats.reasoning: Quiet Tuesday evening; Layla cooks and eats at home, reflecting on the studio day and planning tomorrow.

beats.activity: Film photography work: process negatives and organize contact sheets from recent Alki shoot.
beats.reasoning: Low energy day (45/100 carryover) paired with rainy evening makes darkroom/archival work ideal; aligns with her photography passion and wind-down rhythm.
Productive day in studio, manageable evening; 8.5-hour sleep after a long work day recharges for Wednesday's fair prep push.

diary:
Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle and knew it was a studio day. The SR-520 closure meant taking the long way through local roads, but honestly, I didn't mind—gave me time to think about the new forms I wanted to try. The studio felt especially quiet this morning. I threw for hours, losing myself in the clay the way I always do. There's something about Tuesday mornings when the world's still sleepy.

Grabbed lunch at Molly Moon's to stay out of the rain, watched it hammer against the windows while I ate. Back to the studio for the afternoon push—got three new pieces prepped for the weekend fair. My hands were tired but satisfied by the time I left.

Home now, processed some negatives from last week's Alki shoot while the rain cleared. There's a photo of the beach at low tide I keep coming back to. The light caught something I wasn't expecting. Small day, really, but full.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure could've derailed everything, but I planned around it early—checked weather with my espresso, mapped the Fremont route before leaving. I leaned into the quiet Tuesday energy rather than fight it. The studio work felt urgent because the fair's this weekend, so I prioritized wheel-throwing and glaze testing over anything else. Lunch was strategic (stay dry, eat fast, get back). By afternoon, when the rain cleared, my mood shifted lighter. I had enough mental space to dive into the film work, which I saved for evening leisure. The day felt balanced—maker's work, then artist's work. Sometimes the best days aren't about doing everything; they're about doing the right things in sequence.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
